title: Commodity Dependence, 2022–2024 (and historical trend)
definition: Ratio, expressed as a percentage, of commodity exports to total (all allocated) merchandise exports. Allocated exports correspond to the sum of all individual product lines at the three‑digit level.
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to one decimal place

title: Three Leading Commodity Exported, 2022–2024
definition: Three leading 3‑digit commodities (SITC Rev.3) exported by individual UNCTAD member States as a share of the country’s total (all allocated) merchandise exports. The percentage displayed at the top of each country profile corresponds to the combined share of these three commodities
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to one decimal place

title: Three Leading Destination Markets, 2022–2024
definition: Three leading destination markets for the commodity exports of individual UNCTAD member States. The EU27 is displayed as a single aggregated partner; therefore, its constituent countries (Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, and Sweden) do not appear individually as trading partners in country profiles. The percentage displayed at the top of each country profile corresponds to the combined share of exports directed to these three main destination markets.
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to one decimal place

title: Dependence by Product Groups, 2022–2024
definition:
Dependence by Product Groups is calculated by expressing the total exports of each product group as a share of the country’s total (all allocated) merchandise exports.                                                                                                                   Product groups are defined as follows:
[0 + 1 + 2 (less 27 and 28) + 4] Agricultural commodities 
[3] Energy commodities 
[27 + 28 + 667 + 68 + 971] Mining commodities                                                         [SITC 5 to 8 less 667 and 68] Manufactured goods
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to one decimal place

title: Commodity Exports, 2022–2024 (and historical trend)
definition:
The indicator is calculated for each country and for each geographical subregion, the latter being obtained by summing the commodity exports of all individual member States within the subregion.                                                      [A11] Commodity export data include data for the following:
[0] Food and live animals
[1] Beverages and tobacco
[2] Crude materials, inedible, except fuels
[3] Energy commodities
[4] Animal and vegetable oils, fats and waxes
[667] Pearls, precious and semi-precious stones
[68] Non-ferrous metals
[971] Gold, non-monetary (excl. gold ores and concentrates)                                    The composition of these subregional groupings can be consulted in the list of countries and other classifications provided in the sources.
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to the nearest whole number

title: Commodity Price Indices · 1995 to 2026
definition: Monthly indices based on nominal US dollars
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to one decimal place.  The composition of the groups for each index can be consulted directly on the World Bank website.

title: Historical Commodity Imports, 1995–2024
definition:
[A11] Commodity import data include data for the following:
[0] Food and live animals
[1] Beverages and tobacco
[2] Crude materials, inedible, except fuels
[3] Energy commodities
[4] Animal and vegetable oils, fats and waxes
[667] Pearls, precious and semi-precious stones
[68] Non-ferrous metals
[971] Gold, non-monetary (excl. gold ores and concentrates)
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to the nearest whole number

title: Historical Food Imports, 1995–2024
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to the nearest whole number

title: Historical Energy Imports, 1995–2024
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to the nearest whole number

title: Net Food Imports, 2012–2014 and 2022–2024
definition: [SITC 0 + 22 + 4] Food, basic imports minus [SITC 0 + 22 + 4] Food, basic exports expressed as a share of the country’s total (all allocated) merchandise imports
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to one decimal place. The chart tindicates net food-importing countries with a positive symbol

title: Net Energy Imports, 2012–2014 and 2022–2024
definition: [SITC 3] Energy commodities imports minus [SITC 3] Energy commodities exports expressed as a share of the country’s total (all allocated) merchandise imports
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to one decimal place. The chart tindicates net energy-importing countries with a positive symbol

title: Constant GDP, 2024
definition: GDP data in constant prices (US dollars) are obtained by converting GDP in national currency using the annual period‑average exchange rate of the base year for all years.
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to the nearest whole number

title: Constant GDP per capita, 2024
definition: GDP per capita data in constant prices in US dollars are obtained by converting GDP in national currency using the annual period‑average exchange rate of the base year for all years divided by the total population
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to the nearest whole number

title: Population,  2024
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to the nearest whole number

title: Human Development Index (rank, value, and category),  2023
unit: Index value, rank and HDI category
definition:
The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of average
achievement in key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy
life, being knowledgeable and have a decent standard of living. The HDI is the
geometric mean of normalized indices for each of the three dimensions. Index
categories have fixed cut-off points calculated using the quartiles from the
distributions of the component indicators, as follows: an index value of less
than 0.550 refers to a low level of human development; 0.550–0.699, a medium
level of human development; 0.700–0.799, a high level of human development;
and 0.800 or greater, a very high level of human development
comments: Values for this indicator are rounded to three digits after the period

title: Employment to population, 2024
definition: Employment to population ratio, 15+, total (%) (modeled ILO estimate)
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to one decimal place. Reference year is 2024 excepted for Lebanon (2023), South Sudan (2023), State of Palestine (the) (2022), Sudan (the) (2022), and Ukraine (2021)

title: Female employment, 2024
definition: Employment to population ratio, 15+, female (%) (modeled ILO estimate). SL.EMP.TOTL.SP.FE.ZS.
comments: Data for this indicator are rounded to one digit after the period. Reference year is 2024 except for: Lebanon (2023), South Sudan (2023), State of Palestine (the) (2022), Sudan (the) (2022), Ukraine (2021)
